generatedSentence: Amidst the sterile dorm and its perceived academic neutrality, Aris felt a profound loneliness, an exception to the norm, studying an otherworldly artwork, a shimmering rectangle with harmonic frequencies. This glowing artifact, a unique waymark, signaled a critical juncture for his genomes research, an earmark for perpetuity. He uniquely believed it was the bedrock of a new science, a truth he was gathered to undergo, despite the loathly skepticism from fellow musketeers, knowing jackshit of its true power. The round of corporate rivalry erupted, threatening a boycott of the deep-space oil rig Aris needed, fueling dismay among his allies. A notorious competitor, a veritable kingpin of industrial espionage, used influenza as a cover for a deliberate cutback in life support. Aris implored for reinforcement, sensing an uncanny attempt to victimize his team. Valves were being vacuumed, and life support systems were being tampered with, a neverending modification of an inscribe plot by a knobhead who sought to obfuscate the truth and lambast Aris's every move. The climax was a kinetic blur: Aris, a true maverick, confronted the kingpin on the collapsing oil rig, avoiding the loathly vixens and their cranks. With a single, decidedly desperate act, he triggered the accelerator, unleashing a microscopic pulse that made the bellows hug the structural vertebrae. The dosage of energy, however, was unpredictable; a lunatic n-th degree reaction. His intent was to pacify, but the resulting chaotic happening caused the platform to round and creak, its ankles threatening to give way, as the kingpin offered a carrot of a deal. In the aftermath, the theatre of the cosmos brightly displayed new artwork of his design, a success that earned favorable reviews. His novels of scientific discourse were devoured, even by those who had hoarding tendencies, their once jingoistic vocabularies now rephrase with wonder. He realized whichever path he chose, the truth would shine, shedding light on the undiscovered. The old paperwork and its warnings were but dummies now, a distant echo of necrophobia. Aris, no longer needing to obfuscate his findings, was free to wander, his once ginger ale fueled giggles turning into genuine joy, alive and free of any earlock, having achieved his ideal.
panelSentences: Amidst the sterile dorm and its perceived academic neutrality, Aris felt a profound loneliness, an exception to the norm, studying an otherworldly artwork, a shimmering rectangle with harmonic frequencies. This glowing artifact, a unique waymark, signaled a critical juncture for his genomes research, an earmark for perpetuity. He uniquely believed it was the bedrock of a new science, a truth he was gathered to undergo, despite the loathly skepticism from fellow musketeers, knowing jackshit of its true power.

The round of corporate rivalry erupted, threatening a boycott of the deep-space oil rig Aris needed, fueling dismay among his allies. A notorious competitor, a veritable kingpin of industrial espionage, used influenza as a cover for a deliberate cutback in life support. Aris implored for reinforcement, sensing an uncanny attempt to victimize his team. Valves were being vacuumed, and life support systems were being tampered with, a neverending modification of an inscribe plot by a knobhead who sought to obfuscate the truth and lambast Aris's every move.

The climax was a kinetic blur: Aris, a true maverick, confronted the kingpin on the collapsing oil rig, avoiding the loathly vixens and their cranks. With a single, decidedly desperate act, he triggered the accelerator, unleashing a microscopic pulse that made the bellows hug the structural vertebrae. The dosage of energy, however, was unpredictable; a lunatic n-th degree reaction. His intent was to pacify, but the resulting chaotic happening caused the platform to round and creak, its ankles threatening to give way, as the kingpin offered a carrot of a deal.

In the aftermath, the theatre of the cosmos brightly displayed new artwork of his design, a success that earned favorable reviews. His novels of scientific discourse were devoured, even by those who had hoarding tendencies, their once jingoistic vocabularies now rephrase with wonder. He realized whichever path he chose, the truth would shine, shedding light on the undiscovered. The old paperwork and its warnings were but dummies now, a distant echo of necrophobia. Aris, no longer needing to obfuscate his findings, was free to wander, his once ginger ale fueled giggles turning into genuine joy, alive and free of any earlock, having achieved his ideal.
rawPanelSentences: Amidst the sterile **dorm** and its perceived academic **neutrality**, Aris felt a profound **loneliness**, an **exception** to the norm, studying an **otherworldly artwork**, a shimmering **rectangle** with **harmonic** frequencies. This glowing artifact, a unique **waymark**, signaled a critical **juncture** for his **genomes** research, an **earmark** for **perpetuity**. He **uniquely** believed it was the **bedrock** of a new science, a truth he was **gathered** to **undergo**, despite the **loathly** skepticism from fellow **musketeers**, knowing **jackshit** of its true power.

The **round** of corporate **rivalry** erupted, threatening a **boycott** of the deep-space **oil rig** Aris needed, fueling **dismay** among his allies. A **notorious** competitor, a veritable **kingpin** of industrial espionage, used **influenza** as a cover for a deliberate **cutback** in life support. Aris **implored** for **reinforcement**, sensing an **uncanny** attempt to **victimize** his team. Valves were being **vacuumed**, and life support systems were being tampered with, a **neverending modification** of an **inscribe** plot by a **knobhead** who sought to **obfuscate** the truth and **lambast** Aris's every move.

The **climax** was a **kinetic** blur: Aris, a true **maverick**, confronted the **kingpin** on the collapsing **oil rig**, avoiding the **loathly** **vixens** and their **cranks**. With a single, **decidedly** desperate act, he triggered the **accelerator**, unleashing a **microscopic** pulse that made the **bellows** **hug** the structural **vertebrae**. The **dosage** of energy, however, was unpredictable; a **lunatic** **n**-th degree reaction. His intent was to **pacify**, but the resulting chaotic **happening** caused the platform to **round** and creak, its **ankles** threatening to give way, as the **kingpin** offered a **carrot** of a deal.

In the aftermath, the **theatre** of the cosmos **brightly** displayed new **artwork** of his design, a success that earned **favorable reviews**. His **novels** of scientific **discourse** were devoured, even by those who had **hoarding** tendencies, their once **jingoistic** **vocabularies** now **rephrase** with wonder. He realized **whichever** path he chose, the truth would shine, shedding light on the **undiscovered**. The old **paperwork** and its **warnings** were but **dummies** now, a distant echo of **necrophobia**. Aris, no longer needing to **obfuscate** his findings, was free to **wander**, his once **ginger ale** fueled **giggles** turning into genuine joy, **alive** and free of any **earlock**, having achieved his **ideal**.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. A lone figure, wearing a glowing cybernetic arm and a hooded trench coat, with glowing data-stream eyes. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist Aris Thorne, illuminated by the pulsating glow of an otherworldly, rectangular artifact, examines its intricate patterns on a holographic display, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist narrowly dodges a barrage of laser fire from a shadowy, armored corporate rival on a crumbling, futuristic oil rig, mid-shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Aris, with his cybernetic arm outstretched, unleashes a wave of kinetic energy, causing structural beams to buckle and explode in a dynamic action pose, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Aris stands triumphantly on a re-purposed platform, looking out at a newly terraformed cosmic landscape, a sense of serene discovery on his face, long shot.

definitions.definition: Loathsome; hideous.
The act of boycotting.
To abstain, either as an individual or a group, from using, buying, or dealing with someone or some organization as an expression of protest.
A vegetable with a nutritious, juicy, sweet root that is often orange in colour, Daucus carota, especially the subspecies sativus in the family Apiaceae.
A shade of orange similar to the flesh of most carrots (also called carrot orange).
Any motivational tool.
To treat (an animal pelt) with a solution of mercuric nitrate as part of felt manufacture.
A spontaneous or unposed photograph.
definitions.example: His portraits looked stiff and formal but his candids showed life being lived.

definitions.definition: Impartial and free from prejudice.
Straightforward, open and sincere.
Not posed or rehearsed.
Unspecified or unknown (unlocated) place or location.
In an uncertain or unspecified location.
definitions.example: I must have left my glasses somewhere.

definitions.definition: To an uncertain or unspecified location.
definitions.example: He plans to go somewhere warm for his vacation.

definitions.definition: The state or quality of being neutral; the condition of being unengaged in contests between others; state of taking no part on either side.
Indifference in quality; a state neither very good nor bad.
: The quality or state of being neutral.
The condition of a nation or government which refrains from taking part, directly or indirectly, in a war between other powers.
Those who are neutral; a combination of neutral powers or states.
A foot soldier armed with a musket.
In 17th- and 18th-century France, a member of the royal household bodyguard.
A comrade or fellow.
The act, process, or state of reinforcing or being reinforced.
A thing that reinforces.
(in the plural) Additional troops or materiel sent to support a military action.
The process whereby a behavior with desirable consequences comes to be repeated.
An ongoing relationship between (usually two) rivals who compete for superiority.
definitions.example: James and Polly have a bitter rivalry due to the latter's inclusion in the Tea Time Teaser.

definitions.definition: The characteristic of being a rivalrous good, such that it can be consumed or used by only one person at a time.
definitions.example: Common goods are those which are rivalrous in consumption but non-excludable

definitions.definition: Any competition between two or more things or factors.
definitions.example: Binocular rivalry is a visual phenomenon in which perception alternates between different images presented to each eye.

definitions.definition: To write or cut (words) onto (something, especially a hard surface, or a book to be given to another person); to engrave.
definitions.example: I inscribed my name into the book.

definitions.definition: To draw a circle, sphere, etc. inside a polygon, polyhedron, etc. and tangent to all its sides.
The complete genetic information (either DNA or, in some viruses, RNA) of an organism.
To collect; normally separate things.
definitions.example: I've been gathering ideas from the people I work with.

definitions.definition: To bring parts of a whole closer.
definitions.example: She gathered the shawl about her as she stepped into the cold.

definitions.definition: To infer or conclude; to know from a different source.
definitions.example: From his silence, I gathered that things had not gone well.

definitions.definition: (of a boil or sore) To be filled with pus
definitions.example: Salt water can help boils to gather and then burst.

definitions.definition: To collect molten glass on the end of a tool.
To gain; to win.
Focused, centered, achieving a state of shared mysticism.
A place where things join, a junction.
A critical moment in time.
definitions.example: We're at a crucial juncture in our relationship.

definitions.definition: The manner of moving (transition) or mode of relationship between two consecutive sounds; a suprasegmental phonemic cue, by which a listener can distinguish between two otherwise identical sequences of sounds that have different meanings.
To bring peace to (a place or situation), by ending war, fighting, violence, anger or agitation.
To appease (someone).
A mark or deformation of the ear of an animal, intended to indicate ownership.
The designation of specific projects in appropriations of funding for general programs.
A mark for identification; a distinguishing mark.
To mark (as of sheep) by slitting the ear.
(by extension) To specify or set aside for a particular purpose, to allocate.
definitions.example: You can donate to the organization as a whole, or you can earmark your contribution for a particular project.

definitions.definition: Of, concerned with, or preoccupied with a different world than that of the tangible here and now, such as a heavenly, spiritual, or imaginary world.
Not belonging to the real world; unnatural; odd and unfamiliar.
The release of an arrow.
A state of laxity or indulgence; unrestrained freedom, abandonment.
All play other than set pieces (scrums and line-outs).
Freedom from restraint.
A letting go; discharge.
To let loose, to free from restraints.
To unfasten, to loosen.
To make less tight, to loosen.
Of a grip or hold, to let go.
To shoot (an arrow)
To set sail.
To solve; to interpret.
Not fixed in place tightly or firmly.
definitions.example: This wheelbarrow has a loose wheel.

definitions.definition: Not held or packaged together.
definitions.example: 'You can buy apples in a pack, but they are cheaper loose.

definitions.definition: Not under control.
definitions.example: The dog is loose again.

definitions.definition: Not fitting closely
definitions.example: 'I wear loose clothes when it is hot.

definitions.definition: Not compact.
definitions.example: 'It is difficult walking on loose gravel.

definitions.definition: Relaxed.
definitions.example: She danced with a loose flowing movement.

definitions.definition: Not precise or exact; vague; indeterminate.
definitions.example: a loose way of reasoning

definitions.definition: Indiscreet.
definitions.example: Loose talk costs lives.

definitions.definition: (somewhat dated) Free from moral restraint; immoral, unchaste.
Not being in the possession of any competing team during a game.
definitions.example: He caught an elbow going after a loose ball.

definitions.definition: Not costive; having lax bowels.
Begin shooting; release your arrows
Angular distance measured west or east of the prime meridian.
Any imaginary line perpendicular to the equator and part of a great circle passing through the North Pole and South Pole.
Length.
A feeling of depression resulting from being alone or from having no companions.
The condition or state of being alone or having no companions.
The state of being unfrequented or devoid of human activity (of a place or time).
A desire to be alone; disposition to solitude.
A female fox.
A malicious, quarrelsome or temperamental woman.
A racy or salacious woman.
A wife who has sex with other men with her husband's consent.

A bent piece of an axle or shaft, or an attached arm perpendicular, or nearly so, to the end of a shaft or wheel, used to impart a rotation to a wheel or other mechanical device; also used to change circular into reciprocating motion, or reciprocating into circular motion.
definitions.example: I grind my coffee by hand with a coffee grinder with a crank handle.

definitions.definition: The act of converting power into motion, by turning a crankshaft.
definitions.example: Yes, a crank was all it needed to start.

definitions.definition: Any bend, turn, or winding, as of a passage.
An ill-tempered or nasty person.
definitions.example: Billy-Bob is a nasty old crank! He chased my cat away.

definitions.definition: A twist or turn of the mind; caprice; whim;
A fit of temper or passion.
(dated in US) A person who is considered strange or odd by others. They may behave in unconventional ways.
definitions.example: John is a crank because he talks to himself.

definitions.definition: (1800s) A baseball fan.
An advocate of a pseudoscience movement.
definitions.example: That crank next door thinks he’s created cold fusion in his garage.

definitions.definition: A twist or turn in speech; word play consisting in a change of the form or meaning of a word.
A sick person; an invalid.
A penis.
To turn by means of a crank.
definitions.example: Motorists had to crank their engine by hand.

definitions.definition: To turn a crank.
definitions.example: He's been cranking all day and yet it refuses to crank.

definitions.definition: (of a crank or similar) To turn.
definitions.example: He's been cranking all day and yet it refuses to crank.

definitions.definition: To cause to spin via other means, as though turned by a crank.
definitions.example: Crank it up!

definitions.definition: To act in a cranky manner; to behave unreasonably and irritably, especially through complaining.
definitions.example: Quit cranking about your spilt milk!

definitions.definition: To be running at a high level of output or effort.
definitions.example: By one hour into the shift, the boys were really cranking.

definitions.definition: To run with a winding course; to double; to crook; to wind and turn.
A highly addictive phenethylamine stimulant drug, similar to cocaine. Its systematic (IUPAC) name is (S)-N-methyl-1-phenylpropan-2-amine.
To go or move under or beneath.
To experience; to pass through a phase.
definitions.example: The project is undergoing great changes.

definitions.definition: To suffer or endure; bear with.
definitions.example: She had to undergo surgery because of her broken leg.

definitions.definition: In a unique manner.
definitions.example: a uniquely gifted musician

definitions.definition: A painting, drawing, sculpture or other piece of creative, visual art
Artistic work.
(reprographics) The graphical elements to be included in a reproduced work.
definitions.example: The text has been reviewed, but the photographer hasn't delivered some of the artwork.

definitions.definition: So as to form a circle or trace a circular path, or approximation thereof.
definitions.example: High above, vultures circled around.

definitions.definition: So as to surround or be near.
definitions.example: Everybody please gather around.

definitions.definition: Nearly; approximately; about.
definitions.example: An adult elephant weighs around five tons.

definitions.definition: From place to place.
definitions.example: Look around and see what you find.

definitions.definition: From one state or condition to an opposite or very different one; with a metaphorical change in direction; bringing about awareness or agreement.
definitions.example: He used to stay up late but his new girlfriend changed that around.

definitions.definition: (with turn, spin etc.) So as to partially or completely rotate; so as to face in the opposite direction.
definitions.example: She spun around a few times.

definitions.definition: Used with verbs to indicate repeated or continuous action, or in numerous locations or with numerous people.
definitions.example: I asked around, and no-one really liked it.

definitions.definition: Used with certain verbs to suggest unproductive activity.
Defining a circle or closed curve containing a thing.
definitions.example: I planted a row of lilies around the statue.

definitions.definition: (of abstract things) Centred upon; surrounding.
definitions.example: There has been a lot of controversy around the handling of personal information.

definitions.definition: Following the perimeter of a specified area and returning to the starting point.
definitions.example: She went around the track fifty times.

definitions.definition: Following a path which curves near an object, with the object on the inside of the curve.
definitions.example: The road took a brief detour around the large rock formation, then went straight on.

definitions.definition: Near; in the vicinity of.
definitions.example: I don't want you around me.

definitions.definition: At or to various places within.
definitions.example: She went around the office and got everyone to sign the card.

definitions.definition: A circular or spherical object or part of an object.
A circular or repetitious route.
definitions.example: The guards have started their rounds; the prisoner should be caught soon.

definitions.definition: A general outburst from a group of people at an event.
definitions.example: The candidate got a round of applause after every sentence or two.

definitions.definition: A song that is sung by groups of people with each subset of people starting at a different time.
A serving of something; a portion of something to each person in a group.
definitions.example: They brought us a round of drinks about every thirty minutes.

definitions.definition: A single individual portion or dose of medicine.
One sandwich (two full slices of bread with filling).
A long-bristled, circular-headed paintbrush used in oil and acrylic painting.
A firearm cartridge, bullet, or any individual ammunition projectile. Originally referring to the spherical projectile ball of a smoothbore firearm. Compare round shot and solid shot.
One of the specified pre-determined segments of the total time of a sport event, such as a boxing or wrestling match, during which contestants compete before being signaled to stop.
A stage, level, set of events in a game
(drafting, CAD) A rounded relief or cut at an edge, especially an outside edge, added for a finished appearance and to soften sharp edges.
A strip of material with a circular face that covers an edge, gap, or crevice for decorative, sanitary, or security purposes.
definitions.example: All furniture in the nursery had rounds on the edges and in the crevices.

definitions.definition: (butchery) The hindquarters of a bovine.
A rung, as of a ladder.
A crosspiece that joins and braces the legs of a chair.
A series of changes or events ending where it began; a series of like events recurring in continuance; a cycle; a periodical revolution.
definitions.example: the round of the seasons    a round of pleasures

definitions.definition: A course of action or conduct performed by a number of persons in turn, or one after another, as if seated in a circle.
A series of duties or tasks which must be performed in turn, and then repeated.
A circular dance.
Rotation, as in office; succession.
A general discharge of firearms by a body of troops in which each soldier fires once.
An assembly; a group; a circle.
definitions.example: a round of politicians

definitions.definition: A brewer's vessel in which the fermentation is concluded, the yeast escaping through the bunghole.
A vessel filled, as for drinking.
A round-top.
A round of beef.
To shape something into a curve.
definitions.example: The carpenter rounded the edges of the table.

definitions.definition: To become shaped into a curve.
(with "out") To finish; to complete; to fill out.
definitions.example: She rounded out her education with only a single mathematics class.

definitions.definition: To approximate a number, especially a decimal number by the closest whole number.
definitions.example: Ninety-five rounds up to one hundred.

definitions.definition: To turn past a boundary.
definitions.example: Helen watched him until he rounded the corner.

definitions.definition: To turn and attack someone or something (used with on).
definitions.example: As a group of policemen went past him, one of them rounded on him, grabbing him by the arm.

definitions.definition: To advance to home plate.
definitions.example: And the runners round the bases on the double by Jones.

definitions.definition: To go round, pass, go past.
To encircle; to encompass.
To grow round or full; hence, to attain to fullness, completeness, or perfection.
To do ward rounds.
To go round, as a guard; to make the rounds.
To go or turn round; to wheel about.
(physical) Shape.
Complete, whole, not lacking.
definitions.example: The baker sold us a round dozen.

definitions.definition: (of a number) Convenient for rounding other numbers to; for example, ending in a zero.
definitions.example: One hundred is a nice round number.

definitions.definition: Pronounced with the lips drawn together; rounded.
Outspoken; plain and direct; unreserved; not mincing.

definitions.definition: Finished; polished; not defective or abrupt; said of authors or their writing style.
Consistent; fair; just; applied to conduct.
Large in magnitude.

Vaulted.
To say or write something with different wording.
A close friend; especially used as a form of address.

definitions.definition: A child.
To beg urgently or earnestly.
To call upon or pray to earnestly; to entreat.
Anything surgically implanted in the body, such as a tissue graft or prosthesis, particularly breast implants.
(travel) A representative of a travel company, working within the office of a large client and exclusively dealing with that client.
To fix firmly or set securely or deeply.
To insert (something) surgically into the body.
Of an embryo, to become attached to and embedded in the womb.
To reside in a dorm.

definitions.definition: A room containing a number of beds (and often some other furniture and/or utilities) for sleeping, often applied to student and backpacker accommodation of this kind.
A building or part of a building which houses students, soldiers, monks etc. who sleep there and use communal further facilities.
A dormitory town.

A close embrace, especially when charged with such an emotion as represented by: affection, joy, relief, lust, anger, agression, compassion, and the like, as opposed to being characterized by formality, equivocation or ambivalence (a half-embrace or "little hug").
A particular grip in wrestling.
To crouch; huddle as with cold.
To cling closely together.
To embrace by holding closely, especially in the arms.

definitions.definition: To stay close to (the shore etc.)
To hold fast; to cling to; to cherish.
A component frequency of the signal of a wave that is an integer multiple of the fundamental frequency.
The place where, on a bowed string instrument, a note in the harmonic series of a particular string can be played without the fundamental present.
One of a class of functions that enter into the development of the potential of a nearly spherical mass due to its attraction.
One's child.
Pertaining to harmony
Pleasant to hear; harmonious; melodious
Used to characterize various mathematical entities or relationships supposed to bear some resemblance to musical consonance
definitions.example: The harmonic polar line of an inflection point of a cubic curve is the component of the polar conic other than the tangent line.

definitions.definition: Recurring periodically
Exhibiting or applying constraints on what vowels (e.g. front/back vowels only) may be found near each other and sometimes in the entire word.
Of or relating to a generation an even number of generations distant from a particular person.
A diurnal predatory bird of the family Accipitridae, smaller than an eagle.

An advocate of aggressive political positions and actions.
An uncooperative or purely-selfish participant in an exchange or game, especially when untrusting, acquisitive or treacherous. Refers specifically to the Prisoner's Dilemma, alias the Hawk-Dove game.
To hunt with a hawk.
To make an attack while on the wing; to soar and strike like a hawk.

A map coordinate stored within a satellite navigation system
Nothing, (lack of) anything (i.e. lack of anything of value)
definitions.example: You [don't] know jack shit about politics.

definitions.definition: The quality or state of being perpetual; endless duration; uninterrupted existence.
Something that is perpetual.

A limitation intended to be unalterable and of indefinite duration; a disposition of property which attempts to make it inalienable beyond certain limits fixed or conceived as being fixed by the general law.
An annuity in which the periodic payments begin on a fixed date and continue indefinitely.
The act of excepting or excluding; exclusion; restriction by taking out something which would otherwise be included, as in a class, statement, rule.
That which is excepted or taken out from others; a person, thing, or case, specified as distinct, or not included.

An objection; cavil; dissent; disapprobation; offense; cause of offense; — usually followed by to or against.
An interruption in normal processing, typically caused by an error condition, that can be handled by another part of the program.
A rig, consisting of platform and machinery, used to drill for oil and other petroleum products like natural gas.
To clean (something) with a vacuum cleaner.
To use a vacuum cleaner.
To optimise a database or database table by physically removing deleted tuples.
Having been cleaned with a vacuum cleaner; hoovered.
A device that controls the flow of a gas or fluid through a pipe.
A device that admits fuel and air into the cylinder of an internal combustion engine, or one that allows combustion gases to exit.
One or more membranous partitions, flaps, or folds, which permit the passage of the contents of a vessel or cavity in one direction, but stop or control the flow in the opposite direction

definitions.definition: One of the leaves of a folding-door, or a window-sash.
A vacuum tube.
One of the pieces into which certain fruits naturally separate when they dehisce.
A small portion of certain anthers, which opens like a trapdoor to allow the pollen to escape, such as in the barberry.

One of the two similar portions of the shell of a diatom.
To control (flow) by means of a valve.
A situation in which the king is directly threatened by an opposing piece.
An inspection or examination.
definitions.example: I don't know if she will be there, but it's worth a check.

definitions.definition: A control; a limit or stop.
definitions.example: The castle moat should hold the enemy in check.

definitions.definition: A mark (especially a checkmark: ✓) used as an indicator.
definitions.example: Place a check by the things you have done.

definitions.definition: An order to a bank to pay money to a named person or entity.
definitions.example: I was not carrying cash, so I wrote a check for the amount.

definitions.definition: A bill, particularly in a restaurant.
definitions.example: I summoned the waiter, paid the check, and hurried to leave.

definitions.definition: A maneuver performed by a player to take another player out of the play.
definitions.example: The hockey player gave a good hard check to obtain the puck.

definitions.definition: A token used instead of cash in gaming machines, or in gambling generally.
A lengthwise separation through the growth rings in wood.
A mark, certificate or token by which errors may be prevented, or a thing or person may be identified.
definitions.example: a check given for baggage

definitions.definition: The forsaking by a hawk of its proper game to follow other birds.
A small chink or crack.
A reduction of some sort in an existing program.
Maneuver where the surfer turns and surfs back towards where the wave is breaking.
(roofing) Solvent-thinned bitumen used in cold process roofing adhesives, cements and coatings.
An offensive pass played into a position further from the attacking goal line.
(Freud) Something that is simultaneously familiar and strange, typically leading to feelings of discomfort; translation of Freud's usage of the German "unheimlich" (literally "unsecret").
Strange, and mysteriously unsettling (as if supernatural); weird.
definitions.example: He bore an uncanny resemblance to the dead sailor.

definitions.definition: Careless.
Without fail.
definitions.example: slowly but surely

definitions.definition: Certainly, undoubtedly.
definitions.example: Surely, you must be joking.

definitions.definition: With confidence.
definitions.example: His feet were planted surely on the ground.

definitions.definition: One who, or that which, accelerates.
A device for causing acceleration.
A substance which speeds up chemical reactions.
A pedal causing the vehicle to accelerate when it is pressed.
A chemical that reduces development time.
A device that accelerates charged subatomic particles.
A muscle or nerve that speed the performance of an action.
An accelerator key.
A light van to take mails between a post office and a railway station.
Widely known, especially for something negative; infamous.
Having no end or being endless.
The form of existence belonging to a particular object, entity etc.; a mode of being.
The change undergone by a word when used in a construction (for instance am => 'm in I'm)
The result of modifying something; a new or changed form.
The act of making a change to something while keeping its essential character intact; an alteration or adjustment.
definitions.example: Jim's modification to the radio's tuning resulted in clearer sound.

definitions.definition: A change to an organism as a result of its environment that is not transmissable to offspring.
definitions.example: Due to his sunbathing, Jim's body experienced modifications: he got a tan.

definitions.definition: A change to a word when it is borrowed by another language
definitions.example: The Chinese word "kòu tóu" had a modification made to become the English "kowtow".

definitions.definition: A quadrilateral having opposing sides parallel and four right angles.
The glans penis
A fool, idiot
An acute contagious disease of the upper airways and lungs, caused by a virus, which rapidly spreads around the world in seasonal epidemics.
A sudden or complete loss of courage and firmness in the face of trouble or danger; overwhelming and disabling terror; a sinking of the spirits
definitions.example: He looked in dismay at the destruction of the town caused by the hurricane.

definitions.definition: Condition fitted to dismay; ruin.
To cause to feel apprehension; great sadness, or fear; to deprive of energy
To render lifeless; to subdue; to disquiet.
To take dismay or fright; to be filled with dismay.
The administration of a medication etc, in a measured amount; dosing.
The addition of a small measured amount of a substance to something, e.g. sugar to wine.
The measured amount so administered or added; the dose.
To occur or take place.
definitions.example: Let me tell you how it happened.

definitions.definition: To happen to; to befall.
(with infinitive) To do or occur by chance or unexpectedly.
definitions.example: Do you happen to have an umbrella?

definitions.definition: (followed by on or upon) To encounter by chance.
(of a place) Busy, lively; vibrant, dynamic; fashionable.
definitions.example: This is a happening place tonight!

definitions.definition: (of a person or product) Trendy, up-to-the-minute.
definitions.example: He is a real happening guy.

definitions.definition: A rod bent into a curved shape, typically with one end free and the other end secured to a rope or other attachment.
A barbed metal hook used for fishing; a fishhook.
Any of various hook-shaped agricultural implements such as a billhook.
The curved needle used in the art of crochet.
The part of a hinge which is fixed to a post, and on which a door or gate hangs and turns.
A loop shaped like a hook under certain written letters, for example, g and j.
A tie-in to a current event or trend that makes a news story or editorial relevant and timely.
A snare; a trap.
(in the plural) The projecting points of the thighbones of cattle; called also hook bones.
Removal or expulsion from a group or activity
definitions.example: He is not handling this job, so we're giving him the hook.

definitions.definition: A field sown two years in succession.
(authorship) A brief, punchy opening statement intended to get attention from an audience, reader, or viewer, and make them want to continue to listen to a speech, read a book, or watch a play.
(authorship) A gimmick or element of a creative work intended to be attention-grabbing for the audience; a compelling idea for a story that will be sure to attract people's attention.
A finesse.
A jack (the playing card).
A spit or narrow cape of sand or gravel turned landward at the outer end, such as Sandy Hook in New Jersey.
A catchy musical phrase which forms the basis of a popular song.
definitions.example: The song's hook snared me.

definitions.definition: A ship's anchor.
Part of a system's operation that can be intercepted to change or augment its behaviour.
definitions.example: We've added hooks to allow undefined message types to be handled with custom code.

definitions.definition: (Scrabble) An instance of playing a word perpendicular to a word already on the board, adding a letter to the start or the end of the word to form a new word.
A diacritical mark shaped like the upper part of a question mark, as in ỏ.
A háček.
Senses relating to sports.
To attach a hook to.
definitions.example: Hook the bag here, and the conveyor will carry it away.

definitions.definition: To catch with a hook (hook a fish).
definitions.example: He hooked a snake accidentally, and was so scared he dropped his rod into the water.

definitions.definition: To work yarn into a fabric using a hook; to crochet.
To insert in a curved way reminiscent of a hook.
definitions.example: He hooked his fingers through his belt loops.

definitions.definition: To ensnare or obligate someone, as if with a hook.
definitions.example: A free trial is a good way to hook customers.

definitions.definition: To steal.
To connect (hook into, hook together).
definitions.example: If you hook your network cable into the jack, you'll be on the network.

definitions.definition: (usually in passive) To make addicted; to captivate.
definitions.example: He had gotten hooked on cigarettes in his youth.

definitions.definition: To play a hook shot.
To succeed in heeling the ball back out of a scrum (used particularly of the team's designated hooker).
To engage in the illegal maneuver of hooking (i.e., using the hockey stick to trip or block another player)
definitions.example: The opposing team's forward hooked me, but the referee didn't see it, so no penalty.

definitions.definition: To swerve a ball; kick a ball so it swerves or bends.
To engage in prostitution.
definitions.example: I had a cheap flat in the bad part of town, and I could watch the working girls hooking from my bedroom window.

definitions.definition: (Scrabble) To play a word perpendicular to another word by adding a single letter to the existing word.
To finesse.
To seize or pierce with the points of the horns, as cattle in attacking enemies; to gore.
To move or go with a sudden turn.
(interrogative) Which ever; emphatic form of 'which'.
definitions.example: These chocolates all look delicious. Whichever shall I choose?

definitions.definition: Irrespective of the one(s) that; no matter which one(s).
definitions.example: Whichever you pick, be content with it.

definitions.definition: Any or either one(s) that; the one(s) that.
definitions.example: I have a few books; please borrow whichever interest you.

definitions.definition: Any or either one(s).
definitions.example: I don't mind which you have. Take whichever.

definitions.definition: According to or depending upon which one(s).
definitions.example: I'll buy the red shirt or the blue shirt, whichever is cheaper.

definitions.definition: A high-pitched, silly laugh.
Fun; an amusing episode.
definitions.example: The women thought it would be quite a giggle to have a strippergram at the bride's hen party.

definitions.definition: To laugh gently or in a high-pitched voice; to laugh in a silly or giddy way.
definitions.example: The jokes had them giggling like little girls all evening.

definitions.definition: (with "the") An attack of uncontrolled giggling.
definitions.example: She was seized with a bad case of the giggles.

definitions.definition: A non-alcoholic drink flavored with ginger, similar to ginger beer.
To make an epitome of; to shorten; to condense.
To be an epitome of.
A person appointed to office
To perform the functions of some office.
definitions.example: She officiated as registrar at the wedding.

definitions.definition: To serve as umpire or referee.
definitions.example: He's never officiated a cup-final before.

definitions.definition: A pin inserted through holes at the end of an axle or shaft, so as to secure a wheel or shaft-mounted device.
A central cohesive source of stability and security; a person or thing that is critical to a system or organisation.

A device for delivering pressurized air in a controlled quantity to a controlled location. At its most simple terms a bellows is a container which is deformable in such a way as to alter its volume which has an outlet or outlets where one wishes to blow air.
definitions.example: When wood fires were common, so were bellows for helping start them.

definitions.definition: Any flexible container or enclosure, as one used to cover a moving joint.
The lungs.
Flexible, light-tight enclosures connecting the lensboard and the camera back.
That which fans the fire of hatred, jealousy, etc.
To operate a bellows; to direct air at (something) using a bellows.
To expand and contract like a bellows.
To fold up like a bellows; to accordion.
In a timid manner.
To make someone a victim or sacrifice.
To punish someone unjustly.
To swindle or defraud someone.
That has not been discovered; unknown.
That has not yet been discovered; unexplored.
The skeletal joint which connects the foot with the leg; the uppermost portion of the foot and lowermost portion of the leg, which contain this skeletal joint.
To walk.
To cyclically angle the foot at the ankle while pedaling, to maximize the amount of work applied to the pedal during each revolution.
Having life; living; not dead

definitions.definition: In a state of action; in force or operation; existent
definitions.example: to keep the affections alive

definitions.definition: Busy with activity of many living beings; swarming; thronged; busy.

definitions.definition: Sprightly; lively; brisk.
Susceptible; easy to impress; having keen feelings, as opposed to apathy
(as an intensifier) out of all living creatures.
(only used attributively) Having life; that is alive.
definitions.example: The post office will not ship live animals.

definitions.definition: Being in existence; actual

definitions.definition: Having active properties; being energized.
Operational; in actual use rather than in testing etc.
Taken from a living animal.

definitions.definition: Imparting power; having motion.

definitions.definition: Still in active play.

definitions.definition: Seen or heard from a broadcast, as it happens.

definitions.definition: (of a performance or speech) In person.

definitions.definition: (entertainment, performing) Recorded in front of an audience and not having been edited after recording.

definitions.definition: Of firearms or explosives, capable of causing harm.

definitions.definition: (circuitry) Electrically charged or energized, usually indicating that the item may cause electrocution if touched.

definitions.definition: Being a bet which can be raised by the bettor, usually in reference to a blind or straddle.

definitions.definition: Featuring humans; not animated, in the phrases “live actors” or “live action”.
Being in a state of ignition; burning.

definitions.definition: Full of earnestness; active; wide awake; glowing.
definitions.example: a live man, or orator

definitions.definition: Vivid; bright.
North
(grammar) noun
(grammar) neuter gender
Normal
Neutral
No
Shortening of and, used in set phrases like rock-n-roll.

definitions.definition: So small that it can only be seen using a microscope.

definitions.definition: Very small; minute

definitions.definition: Carried out with great attention to detail.

definitions.definition: Able to see extremely minute objects.

definitions.definition: An unbranded range animal.
(by extension) Anything dishonestly obtained.
(by extension) One who is unconventional or does not abide by rules.

A queen and a jack as a starting hand in Texas hold 'em.

Of cattle: unbranded.
Showing independence in thoughts or actions.

The pin at the centre of the triangle of bowling pins (originally the tallest pin in kayles). Sometimes also the headpin at the apex.
The most important person in an undertaking or organization.
The bolt holding together the truck of a roller skate or skateboard.
Relating to motion
Relating to kinesis or motor function
In a manner which leaves little question; definitely, clearly.

definitions.definition: In a decided or final manner; resolutely.
Verbal exchange, conversation.
Expression in words, either speech or writing.
A formal lengthy exposition of some subject, either spoken or written.

definitions.definition: Any rational expression, reason.
An institutionalized way of thinking, a social boundary defining what can be said about a specific topic (after Michel Foucault).
Dealing; transaction.
To engage in discussion or conversation; to converse.
To write or speak formally and at length.
To debate.
To exercise reason; to employ the mind in judging and inferring; to reason.
To produce or emit (musical sounds).
A silent person; a person who does not talk.
An unintelligent person.

definitions.definition: A figure of a person or animal used by a ventriloquist; a puppet.
Something constructed with the size and form of a human, to be used in place of a person.

definitions.definition: A person who is the mere tool of another; a man of straw.
A deliberately nonfunctional device or tool used in place of a functional one.

definitions.definition: A "dummy teat"; a plastic or rubber teat used to soothe or comfort a baby; a pacifier.

definitions.definition: A player whose hand is shown and is to be played from by another player.
A word serving only to make a construction grammatical.

definitions.definition: An unused parameter or value.
definitions.example: If flag1 is false, the other parameters are dummies.

definitions.definition: A feigned pass or kick or play in order to deceive an opponent.
A bodily gesture meant to fool an opposing player; a feint.
To make a mock-up or prototype version of something, without some or all off its intended functionality.
definitions.example: The carpenters dummied some props for the rehearsals.

definitions.definition: To feint.
A temporary fence-like structure built around building work to add security and prevent accidents to the public.
A roofed wooden shield placed over the battlements of a castle and projecting from them.
A billboard.
The action of the verb warn; an instance of warning someone.
Something spoken or written that is intended to warn.
definitions.example: The boss gave him a warning that he would be fired if he did not desist from his behaviour.

definitions.definition: The sound of metal or glass clattering against itself.

definitions.definition: A small piece of metal attached to a musical instrument, such as a tambourine, so as to make a jangling sound when the instrument is played.

definitions.definition: A memorable short song, or in some cases a snippet of a popular song with its lyrics modified, used for the purposes of advertising a product or service in a TV or radio commercial.

definitions.definition: A carriage drawn by horses.
A brief phone call; a ring.

definitions.definition: A jingle shell.
Coin money.
To make a noise of metal or glass clattering against itself.

definitions.definition: To cause to make a noise of metal or glass clattering against itself.
definitions.example: She jingled the beads as she walked.

definitions.definition: To rhyme or sound with a jingling effect.
Routine work involving written documents
Written documents
Excessive paperwork, busy work, red tape.
The act of licking; a stroke of the tongue.

definitions.definition: The amount of some substance obtainable with a single lick.
definitions.example: Give me a lick of ice cream.

definitions.definition: A quick and careless application of anything, as if by a stroke of the tongue.

definitions.definition: A place where animals lick minerals from the ground.

definitions.definition: A small watercourse or ephemeral stream. It ranks between a rill and a stream.
definitions.example: We used to play in the lick.

definitions.definition: A stroke or blow.

definitions.definition: A small amount; a whit.

definitions.definition: An attempt at something.
A short motif.
definitions.example: There are some really good blues licks in this solo.

definitions.definition: A rate of speed. (Always qualified by good, fair, or a similar adjective.)
definitions.example: The bus was travelling at a good lick when it swerved and left the road.

definitions.definition: An act of cunnilingus.
definitions.example: You up for a lick tonight?

definitions.definition: To stroke with the tongue.
definitions.example: The cat licked its fur.

definitions.definition: To lap; to take in with the tongue.

definitions.definition: To beat with repeated blows.
To defeat decisively, particularly in a fight.

definitions.definition: To overcome.
definitions.example: I think I can lick this.

definitions.definition: To perform cunnilingus.
To do anything partially.
(of flame, waves etc.) To lap.
To dress (somebody) in a bib.
To drink heartily; to tipple.
To beep (e.g. a car horn).
In a bright manner.
definitions.example: The sun was shining brightly, making me squint.

definitions.definition: A place or building, consisting of a stage and seating, in which an audience gathers to watch plays, musical performances, public ceremonies, and so on.
A region where a particular action takes place; a specific field of action, usually with reference to war.
definitions.example: His grandfather was in the Pacific theater during the war.

definitions.definition: A lecture theatre.
An operating theatre or locale for human experimentation.
definitions.example: This man is about to die, get him into theater at once!

definitions.definition: A cinema.
definitions.example: We sat in the back row of the theater and threw popcorn at the screen.

definitions.definition: Drama or performance as a profession or art form.
definitions.example: I worked in theater for twenty-five years.

definitions.definition: Any place rising by steps like the seats of a theater.
Any of the small bones which make up the backbone.
A usually alphabetized and explained collection of words e.g. of a particular field, or prepared for a specific purpose, often for learning.
The collection of words a person knows and uses.
definitions.example: My Russian vocabulary is very limited.

definitions.definition: The stock of words used in a particular field.

definitions.definition: The words of a language collectively.

definitions.definition: A range of artistic or stylistic forms or techniques
A work of prose fiction, longer than a novella.
A fable; a short tale, especially one of many making up a larger work.
A novelty; something new.
A new legal constitution in ancient Rome.
An abnormal fear of death or corpses

definitions.definition: A second or subsequent reading of a text or artifact in an attempt to gain new insights.

definitions.definition: An account intended as a critical evaluation of a text or a piece of work.

definitions.definition: A judicial reassessment of a case or an event.

definitions.definition: A stage show made up of topical sketches etc.

definitions.definition: A survey of the available items or material.

definitions.definition: To write a critical evaluation of a new art work etc.; to write a review.

To view or see again; to look back on.
To retrace; to go over again.
In a favorable manner.

A subring closed under multiplication by its containing ring.

Any similar plant of any genus.

definitions.definition: To stray; stray from one's course; err.

definitions.definition: To consider obligatory or required.

Crazed, mad, insane, demented.
